name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
owner: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada
description: Canada's Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act — federal private-sector privacy law. Replacement law (Bill C-27 CPPA) was proposed but did not pass before 2025 election.
relationToCloudSecurity: Applies to cloud-hosted systems within scope.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI systems within scope.

license.notes: Government publication. Verify per-jurisdiction reuse terms.

current_extraction.notes: Authoritative source is Justice Canada's consolidated XML at https://laws-lois.justice.gc.ca/eng/XML/P-8.6.xml (committed as pipeda.xml). extract_pipeda.py parses the XML to produce per-section structured CSV/JSON (69 entries — 51 substantive sections plus 4 amendment-block placeholders). The original PDF and marker output are retained for reference; do not parse from them — XML is preferred.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire source and extract.
